title: Glasgow Prestwick Airport doubles weekly Air China Cargo flights

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Glasgow Prestwick Airport said Air China Cargo has doubled its weekly freighter operation from four flights to eight and added Chengdu Shuangliu International Airport to its existing Guangzhou service. The airport said the extra four Chengdu flights, operated with an A330 freighter, began on 22 November.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Chief executive Ian Forgie said the airline's decision was a strong vote of confidence in Prestwick's team and cargo capacity. He said the previous six months had been among the busiest in the airport's history, with three new scheduled cargo operators arriving and facilities expanded to support sustained growth.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: Air China Cargo established its UK cargo hub at Prestwick in June 2025, and the airport said regular scheduled services from China Southern Air Logistics and Beijing Capital Airlines have also expanded activity. Prestwick said around 200 jobs have been created, while a GBP 1 million investment in equipment and a dedicated cool chain team supported the launch of a Scotland to China seafood export service in September.
